generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent magnet synchronous motor integrated with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl control system for precise actuation within [-90, 90] degree range with safety cell enforcement"
]

instance GearboxAssembly : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Mechanical gearbox component coupled with the permanent magnet synchronous motor to enable precise kinematic control within the MABI MAX 100 serial robot's operational envelope"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance PositionEncoderHighPrecision : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision position encoder providing 0.06mm path repeatability and 0.1mm positional accuracy for kinematic tracking within the robotic system's operational envelope"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionEncoderDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance PositionEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionEncoderHighPrecision
]

instance MotorCurrentDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

instance AngularVelocityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VelocitySensor
]

instance TorqueDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TorqueSensor
]

instance AccelerationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom AccelerationSensor
]

// C5: Additional Sensing Components
instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor capturing input current data for motor control analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
]

instance VelocitySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Angular velocity sensor measuring rotational speed for dynamic behavior analysis"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AngularVelocityDataTransmitted
]

instance TorqueSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Torque sensor measuring output torque for friction analysis and control validation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData TorqueDataTransmitted
]

instance AccelerationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Acceleration sensor capturing dynamic motion characteristics for behavioral modeling"
    DTDFVocab:producedData AccelerationDataTransmitted
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML IBD representing component-connector architecture of the robotic system including motors, gearboxes, and sensors with their interfaces and connectors"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelingEnabler
]

instance StateMachineBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 state machine model describing sensor data processing workflows and robotic system behavioral states"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo StateMachineSimulationEnabler
]

instance PhysicsBasedSimulationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Analytical model derived from physics-based simulations for dynamic system analysis and friction modeling"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationAnalysisEnabler
]

instance SimulinkFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink executable model for friction analysis within robotic joints"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEnabler
]

instance DigitalShadowDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces from physical sensors and IoT devices processed into structured formats for manufacturing analytics"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PositionEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:fromData AngularVelocityDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:fromData TorqueDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:fromData AccelerationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance TimeSeriesSensorTelemetry : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series dataset containing raw and processed sensor telemetry for real-time monitoring and historical analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PositionEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataVisualizationEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML modeling framework enabling functional and logical architecture modeling including Internal Block Diagrams for component-connector perspectives"
    DTDFVocab:enables SysMLArchitectureService
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]

instance StateMachineSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML state machine execution environment for behavioral modeling and simulation of data processing workflows"
    DTDFVocab:enables StateMachineSimulationService
]

instance SimulationAnalysisEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Heterogeneous simulation environment integrating physics-based and control-oriented simulators for model validation"
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationAnalysisService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for detailed behavioral modeling and friction analysis simulations"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionAnalysisService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom data aggregation layer processing raw sensor data into abstracted formats for specific manufacturing analytics purposes"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
    DTDFVocab:enables PassiveDiagnosticsService
]

instance DataVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Dashboard tooling enabling interactive visualization of aggregated metrics and sensor telemetry data"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeVisualizationService
]

instance MQTTDataTransport : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation enabling efficient, lightweight real-time data transmission from physical sensors to digital representation"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataAggregationService
]

// C6: Services
instance SysMLArchitectureService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing static system structure and interface definitions through SysML Internal Block Diagrams for engineering teams"
    DTDFVocab:provides ArchitectureInsight
]

instance DataAggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service automating collection and abstraction of contextual data traces from physical system into structured formats for manufacturing analytics"
    DTDFVocab:provides AggregatedDataInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides PassiveDiagnosticsAction
]

instance StateMachineSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing SysML state machine models to simulate sensor data processing workflows and robotic system behaviors"
    DTDFVocab:provides BehavioralSimulationInsight
]

instance SimulationAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service performing physics-based and control-oriented simulations for dynamic system analysis and validation"
    DTDFVocab:provides SimulationValidationInsight
]

instance FrictionAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing Simulink friction models to predict and analyze joint friction characteristics in robotic systems"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionAnalysisInsight
]

instance RealTimeVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing interactive dashboards for real-time visualization of aggregated sensor metrics and system performance"
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

instance PassiveDiagnosticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service supporting root-cause analysis through historical and real-time data analysis without autonomous corrective actions"
    DTDFVocab:provides DiagnosticsInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance ArchitectureInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Static system structure and interface definitions enabling engineering teams to understand component relationships and operational boundaries"
]

instance AggregatedDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured, purpose-specific data formats enabling manufacturing analytics and performance trend analysis"
]

instance BehavioralSimulationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Simulated behavioral workflows providing understanding of sensor data processing dynamics and system responses"
]

instance SimulationValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Results from physics-based and control-oriented simulations validating model fidelity against physical system behavior"
]

instance FrictionAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analysis of joint friction characteristics enabling identification of performance degradation factors"
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive dashboard visualizations presenting real-time and historical sensor metrics for stakeholder review"
]

instance DiagnosticsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Root-cause analysis findings enabling engineering teams to identify and address performance issues in robotic systems"
]

instance PassiveDiagnosticsAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Human-in-the-loop analysis process using aggregated data traces for identifying friction and other performance anomalies"

]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s temporal aspects are governed by the synchronization requirements between physical and digital components, which are implicitly addressed through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies. While explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements are not detailed in the provided documents, the integration of **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **SysML IBDs**, **AADL**, or **MontiArc**—combined with **behavioral modeling tools** like **MathWorks Simulink** or other simulation frameworks—implies the need for precise temporal alignment. The **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)** methodology further emphasizes layered abstraction, where physical-layer models (e.g., physics-based simulations) must align with higher-level logical and functional abstractions. Latency and granularity are implicitly managed through **state machines in SysML v1.7** or **MontiArc Automaton**, ensuring consistent temporal behavior across components. However, no specific time scales (e.g., real-time, near-real-time, or periodic updates) or synchronization protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or DDS) are explicitly mentioned in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **orchestration and coordination** of the Digital Twin system is framed within **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** frameworks, leveraging **architecture patterns** from ADLs like **MontiArc** or **SysML** to define component interactions, interfaces, and connectors. Resource management is implicitly handled through **layered modeling** (e.g., requirements → functions → logic → physics), where each layer abstracts and refines system behavior. The **integration of simulation tools** (e.g., **Simulink**, other simulation methods) and **general-purpose languages** (e.g., C++, Java) suggests a **modular, service-oriented architecture** where components communicate via well-defined interfaces. However, no explicit **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, service meshes) or **system-wide coordination mechanisms** (e.g., consensus protocols, federated learning) are mentioned. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Kritzinger et al.) differentiates passive data aggregation from active Digital Twin components, implying that the DT system requires **active components** (e.g., interpreters, processors) to enable bidirectional data flow, though the exact orchestration patterns remain unspecified."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. These stages are managed via methodologies like **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, or **SMArDT**, which systematically decompose system complexity. The lifecycle transitions between these stages involve **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)**, which define **component-connector perspectives**, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition. Behavioral modeling is further refined using **state machines (SysML v1.7)** or **MontiArc Automaton**, often integrated with simulation tools like **MathWorks Simulink** or general-purpose languages (e.g., **C++/Java**). Lifecycle governance integrates **heterogeneous stakeholder perspectives** and ensures alignment across modeling notations and tools, emphasizing **interoperability** and **abstraction consistency** across phases. Transition management between stages adheres to **structured MBSE workflows**, ensuring traceability and version control through toolchain integration (e.g., **SysML + Simulink**)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is implicitly governed by the MBSE methodologies (e.g., RFLP, SPES) and the integration of SysML-based architecture models with simulation tools like Simulink, ensuring consistency across abstraction layers. Validation is inferred through the use of **position encoders with specified accuracy (0.1 mm)** and repeatability (0.06 mm), which serve as primary error quantification mechanisms. Uncertainty sources are not explicitly detailed, but the Digital Twin’s active components (e.g., control system integration) are designed to detect deviations via real-time feedback. Quality assurance processes rely on the structured MBSE framework and toolchain to mitigate model-simulation mismatches, though explicit error correction mechanisms are not described."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The document does not provide explicit details on the **technical network infrastructure** for the Digital Twin system. However, it references **communication flows** between physical and digital entities, implying the use of **streaming protocols** (e.g., MQTT for sensor data transmission). No specifics on communication protocols (e.g., OPC UA, Modbus TCP), network topology (e.g., star, mesh), bandwidth/latency requirements, redundancy mechanisms, or security protocols (e.g., TLS, VPN) are mentioned beyond the general context of **heterogeneous data integration** and **automated one-way data flow**."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The document describes the Digital Twin system’s deployment context indirectly through references to **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies and tools like **SysML**, **Simulink**, and **general-purpose languages (C++, Java)** for behavior modeling. It mentions the need to integrate **heterogeneous models and tools** (e.g., simulation environments, architecture description languages) but does not specify hosting platforms (cloud/edge/on-premise), computational/storage requirements, scalability mechanisms, or deployment models. The only concrete deployment-related detail is the use of **local simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) for behavior description, implying on-premise or hybrid deployment for simulation purposes. No details on orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes), availability/reliability assurance, or dashboard deployment are provided."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is addressed through **heterogeneous system modeling** and **interoperability standards** inherent to MBSE methodologies. The document highlights the need to integrate **multiple modeling notations** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) and **tools** (e.g., Simulink, general-purpose languages) to describe system structure and behavior. While no specific **data exchange protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, REST, SOAP) or **interoperability standards** (e.g., ISO/IEC 42010, IEEE 1471) are cited, the **component & connector perspective** of ADLs like **ACME** or **AADL** implies support for standardized interfaces. Dependencies on **external systems** (e.g., enterprise systems, IoT platforms) are not detailed, but the mention of **heterogeneous stakeholders** suggests integration patterns may align with **enterprise architecture frameworks** (e.g., TOGAF, Zachman). No explicit mention is made of **Digital Twin-to-Digital Twin integration** or **cross-domain interoperability** mechanisms."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The provided documents reference a **physical safety cell** (as per [44] MABI robotic arm specifications) to restrict the operational workspace of the robotic system, ensuring operator safety by confining movement to predefined boundaries. Additionally, [42] describes the use of **MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport)** for data streaming between digital twin components, which inherently supports lightweight, publish-subscribe communication for real-time monitoring and control. However, no explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., TLS, OPC UA security), access control mechanisms, or safety-critical standards (e.g., IEC 61508, ISO 13849) is found in the documents. The context does not detail authentication systems, threat mitigation strategies, or fail-safe mechanisms beyond the physical safety cell."
]
